reliability.summary: The 1988 Toyota Supra is known for head gasket issues and oil system reliability concerns; regular maintenance is needed.

recalls.summary: The 1988 Toyota Supra was not subject to any NHTSA safety recalls. The model is not listed in any recall notices from NHTSA.

market_value.summary: The 1988 Toyota Supra's market value ranges from $25,500 to $78,000. The listed price for a 1988 Toyota Supra is $5,900. This price is significantly lower than the market range.

owner_reviews.summary: Long-term reviews of 1988 Toyota Supra with 297,000 miles highlight durability and maintenance costs. Owners often praise the Supra's performance but note high repair expenses. Some report engine and transmission issues at high mileage.

research.verdict: Watch for: rust. No major recalls found. 💰 Priced below market value (~$41,077). Owners generally love this car.

reliability.summary: The 1989 Toyota Supra often faces blown head gaskets and electrical issues. It's generally seen as low-maintenance but can become costly with major repairs. Reliability varies with ownership and maintenance.

recalls.summary: In 1989, Toyota Supra faced a recall due to fuel tank defects. The defect involved improperly welded fuel tanks. The NHTSA mandated replacement of the defective tanks.

market_value.summary: The 1989 Toyota Supra 2 dr Hatchback turbo typically sells for around $24,995. Prices vary based on mileage and condition. Some listings show prices as low as $19,900.

owner_reviews.summary: The 1989 Toyota Supra has long-term reliability issues, especially with blown head gaskets, but can be low maintenance if properly cared for. Owners report mixed experiences, with some finding it dependable.

research.verdict: 1989 Toyota Supra is considered reliable. Watch for: rust, electrical issues. ⚠️ Has recall(s): fuel tank defects. Fair market price. Mixed to negative owner reviews.

research.car_title: 1995 Toyota Supra Veilside | nissan 300zx fairlady mitsubishi 3000gt

reliability.summary: The 1995 Toyota Supra is known for high mileage issues, especially with the engine and suspension. Common problems include worn bushings and leaks in seals. Reliability can vary based on maintenance history.

recalls.summary: The 1995 Toyota Supra was not subject to any recalls. However, the 1997-1998 models faced a recall for Takata airbags. Check NHTSA for any updates.

market_value.summary: The 1995 Toyota Supra's market value is generally around $54,991, but prices vary based on condition and location. Current listings show similar prices for newer models. Consult specific listings for accurate pricing.

owner_reviews.summary: Long-term owners of 1995 Toyota Supras report mixed reliability, with some praising its driving dynamics but noting higher maintenance costs due to BMW-derived components. High mileage owners emphasize careful maintenance for longevity. The Supra's practicality varies with driving conditions.

research.verdict: 1995 Toyota Supra is considered reliable. Watch for: rust. ⚠️ Has recall(s): Takata airbags. Owners generally love this car.

Selling a Manual Supra Mk5 3.0 Premium MT in Absolute White. I'm relocating and I can't bring the car along with me.
I upgraded from an s2000 and the power and refinement of the car has been a dream.
The car has full ceramic coat with Ceramic Pro Gold package, Full Frontal PPF, and updated High End Focal Speakers with sound isolation from road noise while still retaining the beautiful B58 engine sounds.
Only protective and high end audio upgrades. Car is stock mechanically.
Upgrades:
Full Ceramic Coat + Full Frontal PPF installed by SV Auto - Ceramic Pro Gold package with 10 year warranty ($6k w/ receipts)
Sound Deadening from high end audio retailer Caracoustics - Reduced road noise + no isolation from the B58 engine tones. ($6k w/ receipts)
Upgraded to High end Focal Subwoofer and Speakers ($5k w/ receipts)

research.car_title: 2023 Toyota GR Supra 3.0 Premium MT White

reliability.summary: The 2023 Toyota Supra has reported issues with interior rattles, oil consumption, and some owners express concerns about reliability. These problems highlight potential manufacturing or engineering issues.

recall_details: certain 2023 GR Supra vehicles due to loss of motive power
recalls.summary: In 2023, Toyota issued a recall for certain 2023 GR Supra vehicles due to loss of motive power. The recall was conducted by BMW, which manufactures the GR Supra for Toyota. Owners were advised to have the transmission valve body replaced free of charge.

market_value.summary: The 2023 Toyota Supra's market value ranges from $31,311 to $49,807, but a 2026 model listed at $61,000 is above average. The 2025 model's trade-in value is around $51,700 to $54,200.

owner_reviews.summary: Long-term reviews of the 2023 Toyota Supra at 12,500 miles highlight strong performance and reliability, with few issues reported. Owners appreciate its handling and fuel efficiency. Maintenance costs remain low.

research.verdict: 2023 Toyota Supra is considered reliable. Watch for: oil consumption. ⚠️ Has recall(s): certain 2023 GR Supra vehicles due to loss of motive power. ⚠️ Possibly overpriced vs market (~$47,830). Owners generally love this car.

♥ best of [ ? ]

reliability.summary: The 2020 Toyota Supra generally has a reliable B58 BMW engine, but some owners report oil level issues and minor problems like hatch creaking. Maintenance is mostly routine. Some technical service bulletins address specific issues.

recall_details: potential fuel tank leaks and faulty headlamps
recalls.summary: The 2020 Toyota Supra had a recall for potential fuel tank leaks and faulty headlamps. Owners were notified to have repairs done free of charge. For more details, contact Toyota directly.

market_value.summary: The 2020 Toyota Supra market value ranges from $28,767 to $43,757. The 2020 GR Supra Launch Edition starts at $43,000. The 2021 model's pricing varies from $42,800 to $55,585.

owner_reviews.summary: The 2020 Toyota Supra owner review at 51,277 miles praises its performance and safety features. The review highlights the car's handling and overall satisfaction. No major issues reported.

research.verdict: 2020 Toyota Supra is considered reliable. Watch for: transmission problems. ⚠️ Has recall(s): potential fuel tank leaks and faulty headlamps. Fair market price. Owners generally love this car.
